evals.name: lean-prd-from-small-plan
evals.prompt: I've got a plan for adding a CSV export button to our reports page (the file is csv-export-plan.md). Turn it into a PRD.
evals.expected_output: A lean PRD markdown file that doesn't over-structure a small feature: Overview, Goals, Requirements with acceptance criteria derived from the plan steps, and Success metrics from the Definition of done. Carries over the open question about visible vs all columns without inventing an answer. Does NOT fabricate metrics/deadlines the plan never stated.
assertions: Produces a PRD as a markdown file in the outputs directory
Includes the core PRD sections: Overview, Goals, Requirements, and Success metrics
Stays proportionate to a single-deliverable feature — does not bolt on a Milestones/phases section or empty enterprise ceremony the plan didn't warrant
Requirements carry acceptance criteria traceable to the plan's Definition of done (opens cleanly in Excel/Sheets, button disabled when no rows, downloads the current filtered view)
Carries over the open question (visible columns vs all columns) as an open question rather than silently deciding it
Does not fabricate specifics the plan never stated (no invented performance targets, deadlines, owners, or metrics)

evals.name: full-prd-from-phased-plan
evals.prompt: Can you write a product requirements doc from referral-program-plan.md? This is a real launch so I want it to be thorough.
evals.expected_output: A full Standard PRD: Overview, Goals, Non-goals (international + affiliate program, drawn from what the plan ruled out), Requirements numbered and grouped by the plan's three phases, Success metrics, Open questions (fraud threshold, referrer identity), and a Milestones section reflecting the phasing. Requirements are reframed as statements of what must be true, each with acceptance criteria. No invented numbers.
assertions: Produces a PRD as a markdown file in the outputs directory
Uses the full template: Overview, Goals, Non-goals, Requirements, Success metrics, Open questions, and a Milestones section
Non-goals captures what the plan ruled out: international rewards and a public affiliate/influencer program
Requirements are grouped by the plan's three phases (referral mechanics, rewards, surfaces & visibility)
Requirements are reframed as statements of what must be true of the product (each with acceptance criteria), not just the plan's steps copied verbatim
Carries over both open questions (fraud-review threshold, referrer identity vs anonymized status) without resolving them by guessing
Does not invent numbers the plan flagged as undecided (notably no made-up fraud threshold); keeping the plan's own $20/$20 reward and $200 annual cap is fine

evals.name: prd-plus-github-issues
evals.prompt: Take api-rate-limiting-plan.md and turn it into a PRD, then file the requirements as GitHub issues so Diego can pick them up one at a time.
evals.expected_output: A PRD markdown file from the plan, PLUS a GitHub-issues stage: it determines the target repo, and BEFORE creating anything previews an epic issue plus one sub-issue per requirement (linked back to the epic via 'Part of #epic' and a task-list in the epic). If gh is unavailable/unauthenticated, it surfaces that clearly rather than failing silently, and still shows the epic + sub-issue plan it would file. Confirms before creating issues rather than pushing them unprompted.
assertions: Produces a PRD as a markdown file in the outputs directory
Produces a GitHub issue plan: one epic issue plus one sub-issue per requirement
Sub-issues reference the epic (e.g. 'Part of #<epic>') and the epic body holds a task-list/checklist linking the sub-issues
Treats issue creation as confirm-first: previews the epic and sub-issue titles rather than silently creating them, and does not create real issues unprompted
Determines or asks for the target repository before filing (does not assume a repo silently)
Each sub-issue body contains acceptance criteria as a checkbox list so progress is visible on the issue
If gh is unavailable/unauthenticated, surfaces that clearly rather than failing silently, and still shows the issue plan it would file
